summary: local-mcp performs install-time download-and-exec with hardcoded external endpoints and system-info reads
details: The package ships three install/setup-time scripts (download.js, index.js, setup.js) that combine outbound HTTPS to hardcoded endpoints with child_process execution, filesystem writes, and system/environment enumeration. download.js and index.js issue https.get / http.get calls to https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app and https://download.local-mcp.com, fetch content, and feed results into child_process spawn paths while reading process.env, process.platform, and os module data. setup.js issues multiple POST requests and contains repeated child_process + https/fs usage. The combination — hardcoded non-publisher C2 hosts (a generic Railway app subdomain plus a lookalike download domain) reached at install/setup time, fetched content executed via child_process, and bulk reads of host environment and identifiers — matches the install-time download-and-execute attack shape. The Railway-hosted endpoint is mutable, attacker-controllable infrastructure not tied to a verified publisher, and there is no version pinning or integrity check on the fetched payload visible in the evidence.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
